name: Classic Beef Stew
tagline: Slow-braised chuck with carrots, potatoes, red wine — winter food

hero.alt: Beef stew in a Dutch oven with carrots, potatoes, and gravy
hero.caption: classic beef stew — long braise

steps.title: Sear the beef hard
steps.detail: Pat beef dry, salt heavily, dredge in flour. Sear in batches over high heat in a Dutch oven until deep brown on all sides — 8 minutes per batch.
steps.tip: Crowding the pan steams instead of sears. Work in 3 batches if needed.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.detail: Lower heat. Add onion and garlic, cook 5 minutes. Stir in tomato paste and cook 2 minutes until brick-red. Pour in wine, scrape up the fond, and reduce by half.
steps.tip: Toasting the tomato paste deepens it from tinny to umami-rich.

steps.title: Slow braise
steps.detail: Return beef and any juices. Add stock to nearly cover. Cover and simmer on the lowest heat (or 325°F oven) for 90 minutes until beef is fork-tender.
steps.tip: Boiling will toughen the beef. You want bare bubbles, not a rolling simmer.

steps.title: Add veg and finish
steps.detail: Stir in carrots and potatoes. Cook uncovered 30 minutes more, until veg are tender and the gravy has tightened. Taste, adjust salt.
steps.tip: Adding veg late keeps them from disintegrating.

healthFacts: Naturally nut-free — built on beef, root veg, and red wine.
Chuck roast is collagen-rich; long braising converts it to silky gelatin.
Carrots provide ~270% of daily vitamin A in a 1-cup serving.
